category_trends: Sustainable materials becoming default expectation, not premium feature
AI-powered personalization in product recommendations and custom design
Recovery and wellness expanding beyond performance — sleep, nutrition, mental health
Direct-to-consumer digital channels overtaking wholesale partners
Membership and subscription models replacing transactional retail
Hyper-local community activations replacing global mass campaigns
cultural_moments: Summer Olympics 2026 — Milan-Cortina, massive global attention moment
Women's sports viewership continues historic growth trajectory
Mental health in athletics post-Olympic athlete advocacy movement
Gen Z entering prime athletic participation years with different values
AI-generated creative tools democratizing design — authenticity becomes differentiator
emerging_angles: Anti-perfectionism — celebrating messy, real athletic moments over polished hero shots
Micro-communities — city running clubs, park yoga groups, local climbing gyms
Performance for longevity — not peak achievement but sustainable lifelong fitness
Athleticism as activism — using sport to drive social change
Digital-physical hybrid experiences — QR codes on products linking to training content
response.timing_notes: Q1-Q2 2026: Build Olympic narrative. Q3: Back-to-school push with Gen Z focus. Q4: Holiday campaign emphasizing gift-of-health positioning. Sustainability messaging should be year-round baseline, not seasonal.
